items.title: Arrays & Lists
items.description: A Python list backs a stack natively (append/pop at the end). It is the wrong choice for a queue because pop(0) is O(n).

items.title: Linked Lists
items.description: collections.deque is a doubly-linked block list; this is what gives queues O(1) at both ends.

items.title: Heaps & Priority Queues
items.description: Priority queues replace 'oldest first' with 'highest priority first' and are typically built on heaps.

items.title: Graphs
items.description: BFS uses a queue; DFS uses a stack — same algorithm skeleton, different ADT picks the traversal order.
